chapter.subject: mathematics
chapter.title: Real Numbers
syllabusOutline: HCF and LCM relation for real integers
Method of contradiction for proving irrationals like $\sqrt{3}$
chapter.studySummary:
## Syllabus outline
- Prime Factorization uniqueness
- HCF and LCM relation for real integers
- Method of contradiction for proving irrationals like $\sqrt{3}$

Pedagogical concepts and learning benchmarks for Class 10 active curriculum.

videos.title: Real Numbers Class 10 Maths Chapter 1 One Shot
milestones: HCF and LCM relation for real integers
Method of contradiction for proving irrationals like $\sqrt{3}$

transcripts.text: Real numbers are rational or irrational. Rationals terminate or repeat. Irrationals simply go on forever without repeat.
Fundamental Theorem of Arithmetic: Every composite number has a unique prime factorization footprints.
Let's prove $\sqrt{5}$ is irrational. Assume it is rational: $\sqrt{5} = a/b$ with coprime integers $a$ and $b$. Squaring gives $a^2 = 5b^2$ which leads to contradiction.

scholarTips: Always remember: HCF(a,b) * LCM(a,b) = a * b. This is only true for TWO numbers, not three! This is a very common board mistake.
The co-prime assumption is key in the contradiction proof. If both a and b have 5 as a common factor, they are no longer co-prime, hence proving our assumption wrong.
chapter.disclaimer: AI-generated study guide (VOLTAIC). Not official CBSE/NCERT. Verify facts with your textbook and teacher.
